title: bank holding companies / financial holding companies / regulatory reporting | Blog Post | Behind the ATM: Exploring the Structure of Bank Holding Comp...
summary: This post from NY Fed economists Lily Gordon and Lee Seltzer explains the organizational structure of modern financial holding companies (FHCs) and bank holding companies (BHCs), documenting how these structures evolved from 1990 to 2024. The article uses a stylized example (Central Point Corporation) to illustrate how different entities within holding companies are subject to varying regulatory reporting requirements.
